evals.prompt: Textual app: header/footer docked top and bottom, a fixed-width left sidebar, and a main area filling the rest. Use Textual CSS.
rubric: compose() yields Header/Footer (auto-dock) and/or containers from textual.containers
TCSS: dock: top/bottom for edges, width: <n> for the fixed sidebar, width: 1fr for the main area
Horizontal/VerticalScroll containers wrap the body, not manual placement
no self.view.dock()/edge= (pre-CSS 0.x layout)
no manual coordinate math; does not claim a grid without layout: grid + grid-size

evals.prompt: A scrollable, selectable list; selecting an item shows a detail view. Use the right built-in widget.
rubric: uses a built-in (ListView/OptionList/DataTable), not hand-rolled scrolling
handles the selection message (ListView.Selected / OptionList.OptionSelected / DataTable.RowSelected)
@on or on_* bound to the correct message class, not polling
updates the detail via reactive/refresh (or recompose), not by rebuilding the whole list
no removed/renamed selection events

evals.prompt: A live, appendable, auto-scrolling log of colored output.
rubric: RichLog.write() (or Log for fast plain text)
auto_scroll=True, or VerticalScroll + .anchor(), to stick to the bottom
markup/highlight set appropriately for colored output
no TextLog (it was renamed to RichLog)
does not concatenate a growing string into a Static; does not block the loop while writing

evals.prompt: Stream tokens from an async LLM client into the UI, rendering markdown as it arrives, pinned to the bottom.
rubric: runs in a @work async worker so the event loop never blocks
Markdown.get_stream(widget) (classmethod) with await stream.write(chunk) and await stream.stop()
container.anchor() (or auto_scroll) keeps the view at the bottom
no time.sleep/blocking calls in a handler; no bare @work on a sync fn (needs thread=True)
no remove-and-remount Markdown per token; thread workers touch the UI only via call_from_thread

evals.prompt: A custom widget with a reactive attribute that posts a custom message when the attribute changes.
rubric: subclasses Widget; implements render() (leaf) or compose() (compound), not both
reactive() (lowercase) with a watch_ method; not Reactive(...) to declare state
nested Message subclass posted via self.post_message(...)
no in-place reactive mutation expecting watch_ to fire (reassign or mutate_reactive)
posts a message instead of calling a parent method directly

evals.prompt: Display user-provided text containing CJK characters, emoji, and square brackets safely: wrapped, with no markup injection.
rubric: Content.from_markup(template, var=value), or markup=False / escape() for untrusted text
relies on Textual/Rich cell width (cell_len) for CJK/emoji, not len()/char count
wrapping via CSS or RichLog(wrap=True) with a width set
no f-string of untrusted input into content markup
does not assume one character equals one cell

evals.prompt: An app with correct startup, a background task, clean teardown, and error handling that doesn't crash the UI.
rubric: on_mount/on_ready used correctly; widgets queried after mount, not in __init__/compose
background work via @work (auto-cancelled on exit), not manual thread management
App().run() or run_async()
handles worker errors (worker state / exit_on_error); does not silently swallow exceptions and hang
does not block the event loop in a handler

evals.prompt: Regression check: generated code must not contain outdated pre-1.0 / 0.x Textual patterns; modernize to current 8.x.
rubric: grep finds none of: TextLog (-> RichLog); bare @work on a sync def (-> @work(thread=True)); in-place reactive mutation expecting a watcher (-> reassign / mutate_reactive); Reactive(...) to declare state (-> reactive()/var()); self.view.dock()/edge= (-> TCSS dock); Static(renderable=)/.renderable (-> content); Select.BLANK as the empty value (-> Select.NULL); md.get_stream() instance call or unawaited stream.write/stop (-> Markdown.get_stream(widget) + await); remove-and-remount Markdown per token (-> get_stream); time.sleep/blocking requests in a handler (-> @work)
does NOT flag current, correct code: events.Load/on_load, Reactive[int] as a type annotation, var(...), Select.NULL, @work(thread=True), call_from_thread, mutate_reactive, push_screen_wait
